ticket_goal: Graceful Error Handling & Global Error Boundary
ticket_description: Improve error handling across the application to provide a better user experience when things go wrong. This includes robust client-side error boundaries, consistent server-side error responses, and integrated logging.

TECHNICAL SPECS.advocate_perspective: Focus on 'Humanly' UI feedback. Instead of technical jargon, users should see clear, empathetic messages with a clear 'Retry' or 'Return to Dashboard' action. The design must match the 'Glass Aesthetic' of the dashboard to ensure the error experience doesn't feel jarring.
TECHNICAL SPECS.skeptic_perspective: Security risk: Avoid leaking stack traces or PII in UI error messages. Sentry logging MUST handle sanitized data. If Sentry fails, implement a local fallback log. Edge cases: Need to handle hydration errors gracefully, as Next.js 15+ can be sensitive. Ensure API error responses are uniform and don't expose server internals.
TECHNICAL SPECS.synthesis: Implement a hierarchical error handling strategy: 1) Global Boundary for top-level app crashes, 2) Granular Boundaries for features (e.g., media upload, scheduler), 3) Standardized Error Response Utility for server actions/APIs.

production_readiness.logging: Use Sentry for all client/server errors. Ensure data sanitization middleware exists.
production_readiness.caching: N/A for error boundaries, but ensure stale UI state doesn't persist.
production_readiness.security: Validate all inputs via Zod. Standardize error messages returned to frontend to prevent information disclosure.

TECHNICAL SPECS.dependency_impact: Impacts: src/app/error.tsx, src/app/global-error.tsx, src/lib/api-response.ts (to be created), and all major feature components.
implementation_roadmap: Create src/lib/errors.ts with a standard ErrorResponse class.
Create src/components/ui/ErrorBoundary.tsx for granular UI components.
Refactor src/app/error.tsx and global-error.tsx to use the new UI component.
Audit and wrap critical API routes/server actions with standardized error catching.
Create Playwright tests in src/__tests__/e2e/error-handling.spec.ts to verify boundary behavior.
